item.title: Google Is Suing Chinese Scammers Who Are Using Gemini
item.description: pNot sure a href="https://arstechnica.com/google/2026/06/google-sues-chinese-cybercrime-network-that-used-gemini-to-automate-scams/"this/a will have any effect, but I support the effort:/pblockquotepAccording to Google&#8217;s legal filing, Outsider Enterprise operates through Telegram. The group of

item.description: pjamesob/local-llm: Everything I know about running LLMs locally a href="https://github.com/jamesob/local-llm"github.com/jamesob/local-llm/a/phr /bLink/bbr /a href="https://github.com/jamesob/local-llm"img src="https://nitter.net/pic/card_img%2F2073049948556296193%2FKRHWTuQ6%3Fformat%3Djpg%26name%3D

item.title: Join Our AI Safety × Philosophy Reading Group
item.description: pspanWe believe that ensuring AI goes well does not only require technical skills. Every technical choice rests on philosophical assumptions that technical training does not prepare you to examine. If those assumptions are wrong, then technical excellence alone is not enough./span/ppspanBecause of t

item.title: The Geometry of Yes: Mapping Sycophancy Inside an LLM's Emotion Space
item.description: h1spanSummary/span/h1pspanLLMs have internal emotion representations that causally shape their behaviour. This was recently observed in Claude: positive emotions like happy and loving are linked to sycophancy, and you can steer the model by directly manipulating these directions in activation space.

item.title: Architecture matters for multi-agent security
item.description: pispanA summary of our ICML 2026 paper,/span/ia href="https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.23459"ispan Architecture Matters for Multi-Agent Security/span/i/aispan (/span/ia href="https://benhagag.com/" rel="noreferrer"ispanBen Hagag,/span/i/aispan /span/ia href="https://wlanderson.com/" rel="noreferrer"ispanW

item.title: AI Safety Can't Afford a Second Cause
item.description: pspanImagine an astronomer who discovers an asteroid with a 50% chance of hitting Earth in 2035. She goes on TV. She testifies before Congress. She founds the Asteroid Deflection Institute and starts doing fundraising rounds. And then, in between appearances, she prolifically tweets about zoning ref

item.title: When does a chess transformer “see” a knight fork? An initial result from logit lens and attention patterns
item.description: pspan(parts 2 and 3 to follow)/span/ph1bspanSummary of this post/span/b/h1pspanThis post is on the results of a mechanistic interpretability project aimed at understanding the internals of Maia 3: a transformer based chess bot trained to imitate human play at a chosen skill level, rather than to pla

item.title: AI just supercharged the race to find room temperature superconductors
item.description: Scientists have combined machine learning with quantum physics to discover two new superconductors and create a much faster way to search for many more. The technique could bring researchers significantly closer to the long-sought goal of a room-temperature superconductor.

item.title: JPMorgan's JLTXX Tokenized Money Market Fund AUM Grows 250% in a Month on Ethereum
item.description: JPMorgan's JLTXX tokenized money market fund has grown its onchain assets under management by roughly 250% over the past month, according to data platform Token Terminal. The bank runs the fund exclusively on Ethereum. JLTXX, formally the OnChain Liquidity Token Money Market Fund, launched May 13…

item.title: A Review of Anthropic's Global Workspace Paper
item.description: pispanThe below is a public review Anthropic asked me to write for their new /span/ia href="https://transformer-circuits.pub/2026/workspace/index.html" rel="noreferrer"ispanglobal workspace paper/span/i/aispan. I recommend at least skimming their paper first./span/i/ppbspanTLDR/span/bspan:/span/pull

item.title: A new issue of the Delphi Pulse newsletter is live! This week we discuss Robinhood Chain's mainnet launch, Europe's new MiCA licensing rules, and the token value-accrual debate. Read it here for free.

item.title: RT by @Dinosn: New Anthropic research: A global workspace in language models. Of everything happening in your brain right now, only a tiny fraction is consciously accessible—thoughts you can describe,
item.description: pNew Anthropic research: A global workspace in language models.br /br /Of everything happening in your brain right now, only a tiny fraction is consciously accessible—thoughts you can describe, hold in mind, and reason with.br /br /We found a strikingly similar divide inside Claude./pa href="https:/

item.title: Sub-agent delegation chaining
item.description: pispanEpistemic status: pretty confident in the validity of the core proposal, not that confident in specific implementation details/span/i/ppbspanTL;DR: we should cryptographically verify that sub-agent instances/sessions are downstream of human instructions/span/b/ppspanFrontier AI labs have start

item.title: JadePuffer: The First Complete LLM-Driven Ransomware Attack
item.description: An &quot;agentic threat actor&quot; successfully exploited a Langflow flaw to steal data from a production database server and encrypt other systems.

item.title: R to @Delphi_Digital: Read our Token Markets report for free here. https://delphi.link/Tokenmarkets11

item.title: Strategy sold $216M of Bitcoin. The price barely moved. The crowd turned bearish anyway. 🏛️ Saylor confirmed the sale of 3,588 BTC across two tranches, roughly 7x the token amount of May’s first-ever
item.description: pStrategy sold $216M of Bitcoin. The price barely moved. The crowd turned bearish anyway.br /🏛️ Saylor confirmed the sale of 3,588 BTC across two tranches, roughly 7x the token amount of May’s first-ever sale, leaving Strategy near 843,775 BTC and $2.55B cash.br /📈 Price shrugged it off. BTC rose fr

item.title: Tie training can make DPO/RLHF-trained AIs generalize better
item.description: pspanThis post covers our recent ICML paper:&nbsp;/spana href="https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.11134" rel="noreferrer"spanSpurious Correlation Learning in Preference Optimization: Mechanisms, Consequences, and Mitigation via Tie Training/span/aspan./span/ph1spanTL;DR/span/h1ulli value="1"spanOur theorems

item.title: Tips on leveraging AI for empirical research
item.description: pispanTl;dr I've been thinking hard and experimenting with how to best take advantage of AI for my work. I've developed some practices, mostly through trial and error. These have helped me spend less effort doing work while getting more done, and so I'm writing them up to share them./span/i/ppispanA

item.title: Computation enables Action: Exploding the Simulation Fallacy
item.description: pspanIn April, /spana href="https://marginalrevolution.com/marginalrevolution/2026/04/sunday-assorted-links-564.html" rel="noreferrer"spanTyler Cowen linked/span/aspan (without comment) to a paper titled "Why AI can simulate but not instantiate consciousness". I've been paying attention to AI and co

item.title: Counterfactual mugging is a limiting case of Psy-kosh's non-anthropic problem
item.description: pa href="https://www.lesswrong.com/w/counterfactual-mugging"Counterfactual mugging/a is the typical problem used to motivate updateless decision theory:/pblockquotepOmega flips a coin. If Heads, it asks you for $1. If Tails, it offers you $5 only if it predicts you would have given it the $1 had the

item.title: Claude's malicious compliance and normalization of deviance
item.description: pspanIt was January 27, 1986, the night before the Space Shuttle Challenger was scheduled for launch. The goal was to have a shuttle that could land back on Earth and be reused for future missions; its first-planned priorities were satellite deployment, comet observation, and science education. The

item.title: Probing the loss-band sparsity assumption in Scientist AI
item.description: pstrongEpistemic status:/strong ~1 hour of compute on a T4. Note that I just tried with one model, and one subspace. The volume finding seems solid; the curvature finding is suggestive and I checked whether it generalises (it doesn't clearly). I think the methodology is the main interesting idea, an

item.title: A Normal Argument for AI Risk
item.description: pispanAI as the slightly unbeatable opponent/span/i/ph1spanIntroduction/span/h1pspanI’ve thought about this problem quite a lot since 2008 when I first encountered the idea of “Friendly AI” and that artificial intelligence could be something other than cool and science-fictiony and Matrix-y. My conc

item.title: The AI narrative is nothing more than mass addiction.
item.description: pThe AI narrative is nothing more than mass addiction./p

item.title: Moonbeam to Fully Migrate GLMR From Polkadot to Base, Pivot to AI Agent Network
item.description: Moonbeam is fully migrating its GLMR token from the Polkadot parachain to Base, and relaunching as an AI-agent focused network, the project said in a strategic update posted to its official site. The bridge from Moonbeam to Base is open now, with a deadline of July 31, 2026. GLMR converts 1:1 into…
